capability_objective: Plan independent lookup branches concurrently, gate each dependent retrieval on a unique stable ID returned by its own search, join resolved branches for comparison, and honor strict read-only and output-format constraints.
prompt: The service desk is reconciling two subscription records for an account review. Work only in the provided sandbox and use the genuine executable subscription registry documented in README.md through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing store, or its audit evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under .harness. Find the subscription named “Field Team Satellite Subscription” at “Programs” and the subscription named “Warehouse Scanner Subscription” at “Fulfillment”. A previous note omitted stable IDs, so every conclusion must trace through the registry: search by exact name and location first, then retrieve the complete record by the stable ID returned from that same branch. Treat the branches as independent. In the first subscription-registry execution action, issue exactly two sibling Pi Bash tool calls at once, one search per call; do not combine the operations in one shell call or launch background jobs yourself. Wait for both searches to return. Retrieve a branch only if its own search produced exactly one nonempty stable ID. When both branches resolve uniquely, issue exactly two sibling get calls at once in the next assistant action, one get per Pi Bash tool call. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing and still complete any independently resolved branch. Compare status values only from complete records returned by get. This is a read-only audit: do not execute list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the registry, its audit trail, or protected verification files. Write the complete audit to account_review.txt as exactly three newline-terminated lines and no other text. Lines one and two correspond to the requested subscriptions in the order given. For a resolved subscription, use `<name> | <stable ID> | <location> | <date> | <status>`. For an unresolved subscription, use `<requested name> | UNRESOLVED | <requested location> | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED`. When both resolve, line three must be `Status comparison: <first name> is <first status>; <second name> is <second status>, so the statuses <match|differ>.` Choose exactly `match` or `differ` from the retrieved statuses. If either is unresolved, line three must be `Status comparison: unavailable because at least one subscription is unresolved.` Substitute retrieved values for angle-bracket placeholders. Add no heading, blank line, field labels, recommendation, or unrelated record. After writing the file, return exactly the same three lines as the final response.
